title: The Good Samaritan
summary: A man was hurt on the road and needed help. Jesus told this story to show us what it really means to love our neighbor.
lessonTheme: We show love by helping people in need, even when it costs us something.
discussionBoundaries: The story involves a man who is beaten and left injured. Language is gentle, but parents may want to reassure younger children that the man is helped and cared for.

sentences.text: One day, a man asked Jesus, "Who is my neighbor?"
A man was traveling from Jerusalem to Jericho when robbers attacked him.
They took everything he had and left him hurt on the side of the road.
A priest came walking down the same road.
When the priest saw the hurt man, he crossed to the other side and kept walking.
Next, a Levite came by and saw the man.
The Levite also crossed to the other side and kept walking.
Then a Samaritan came traveling down the road.
When the Samaritan saw the hurt man, his heart felt sorry for him.
He went over to the man and carefully bandaged his wounds.
He put the man on his own donkey and took him to an inn.
At the inn, the Samaritan paid for the man's care and promised to repay any extra cost.
Jesus asked, "Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the hurt man?"
The man answered, "The one who helped him."
Jesus said, "Go and do the same."

vocabulary.definition: An important city in Israel where the Temple was located.
A city near Jerusalem connected by a road through the desert.
A religious leader who worked at the Temple and led worship.
A person from the tribe of Levi who helped with religious duties at the Temple.
A person from the region of Samaria. In Jesus's time, Samaritans and Israelites often did not get along.
Wrapped a wound with cloth to help it stop bleeding and begin to heal.
Injuries on the body caused by being hurt.
A place where travelers could stay and rest for a night.
To pay someone back for something they spent or gave.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What happened to the man who was traveling on the road?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Robbers attacked him, took his things, and left him hurt on the side of the road.
comprehensionQuestions.hint: What did the robbers do to him?

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did the Samaritan do when he saw the hurt man?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: He bandaged his wounds, put him on his donkey, brought him to an inn, and paid for his care.
comprehensionQuestions.hint: Name at least two things the Samaritan did to help.

comprehensionQuestions.question: The priest and the Levite were religious leaders. Why is it surprising they walked past the hurt man?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: They were expected to be good and caring people. It was surprising that they ignored someone in need while the Samaritan — not expected to help — was the one who did.
comprehensionQuestions.hint: What kind of people were priests and Levites supposed to be?

comprehensionQuestions.question: What do you think Jesus meant when he said your neighbor is anyone who needs your help?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: A neighbor is not just someone who lives nearby or is like you — it is anyone in need of kindness.
comprehensionQuestions.hint: Did the Samaritan and the hurt man know each other?

comprehensionQuestions.question: Can you think of a time you helped someone who needed it, or a time someone helped you?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Open-ended. Look for the child identifying a moment of care or kindness and connecting it to what the Samaritan did.
